name: Reaction Enthalpy via Hess's Law
description: Obtain a reaction enthalpy either by direct calorimetry or, when the reaction is impractical to measure, by combining tabulated enthalpies of known reactions or formation enthalpies through Hess's law.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Target reaction"]
    N2{"Measurable directly?"}
    N3["Calorimetry: measure q"]
    N4["Select known reactions"]
    N5["Scale + reverse equations"]
    N6["Sum enthalpies (Hess)"]
    N7["Use ΔHf° formation data"]
    N8["ΔH_rxn obtained"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 -->|yes| N3
    N3 --> N8
    N2 -->|no| N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N8
    N2 -->|formation data| N7
    N7 --> N8

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Physical Chemistry, 11th ed. — The First Law (thermochemistry, Hess's law)
sources.authors: Atkins, P.; de Paula, J.; Keeler, J.

sources.title: Gold Book: standard enthalpy of reaction
NIST Chemistry WebBook — enthalpies of formation
sources.authors: Linstrom, P. J.; Mallard, W. G. (eds.)
